response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer (15+ years) but is a poor fit for this specialized role. The position has a hard requirement for native-level Japanese, which the resume does not list at all (only English, Romanian fluent, German basic). It also requires strong C proficiency for UNIX development, Linux kernel source modification, and embedded networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF/SNMP) — none of which appear. His C/C++ experience is limited and dated (~6 months at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis), with the bulk of his career in web/backend (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang). The core domain and language requirements are unmet.
strengths: Extensive, broad software engineering experience across many domains
Some C/C++ exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, embedded thesis work)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing focus
concerns: No Japanese language ability (native-level is a hard requirement)
No embedded networking security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switches)
Minimal and dated C/UNIX and Linux kernel work; career is mostly web/backend

key_factors.factor: Weak/dated C and Linux kernel skills
